title: Isolation of Lipid Droplets from Cultured Cortical Neurons
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fonob","text":"This protocol provides a robust method for purifying lipid droplets from cultured cortical neurons, suitable for lipidomic analysis of constituent lipids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Isolate cortical neurons from P0-P1 pups and culture them in 15 cm dishes (8-10 plates).
Maintain the neurons in standard culture condition for 14-21 days to allow growth of axons and synapse formation.
Treat the neurons with 5 micromolar (µM) KLH45 (dissolved in DMSO) for 24 hours prior to harvesting.

Harvest neurons in 1.5 mL of 0.9 Molarity (M) MEPS buffer supplemented with 1X protease inhibitor cocktail. MEPS buffer composition: PIPES: 35 mM (adjust pH to 7.2 with KOH), EGTA: 5 mM, MgSO₄: 5mM, Sucrose: 0.9 M

Lyse the neurons using the nitrogen cavitation method or Isobiotec Cell Homogenizer (8-10 strokes with 16-micron clearance). Confirm complete lysis under an inverted white-field or phase-contrast microscope.
Centrifuge the lysate at 1800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes to remove unlysed somas, nuclei, and plasma membrane fractions.
Collect the supernatant (called post-nuclear supernatant, PNS) into a fresh tube.
Pour the PNS over 2.5 M MEPS in a clear ultracentrifuge tube (SW41).
Overlay the bottom layer with 2 mL each of 1.2 Molarity (M) , 0.5 Molarity (M) , and 0 Molarity (M) MEPS to establish a step gradient.
Load the ultracentrifuge tube into the SW41Ti rotor and balance it against an identical tube.
Centrifuge the gradient at 174000 x g, 4 °C, 1 hour 30 minutes .
Carefully transfer the ultracentrifuge buckets to ice.
Collect the LDs floating on the top of gradient using a syringe fitted with appropriate needles (25 gauge) or a tube slicer.
Snap-freeze the collected LDs in liquid nitrogen for future use.
Perform western blotting to confirm the purity of the isolated LDs.

Confirm enrichment of LD by Perilipin-2 and contamination (if any) using organelle-specific markers for mitochondria, ER, Golgi, and endosomes. •Appropriate clearance/number of strokes with Isobiotec cell Homogenizer, or pressure during Nitrogen cavitation is critical for effective lysis while preserving organelle integrity. •Extreme care is required during the gradient setup to prevent contamination of the LD layer. •Ensure all ultracentrifuge tubes are properly balanced to avoid damage of rotor/centrifuge. •Cell-Mask Plasma Membrane Stain can be used to confirm absence of unwanted membrane contamination in LDs samples.
creator: Mukesh Kumar
